chapter_title: Operation of Bicycles & Mopeds, Golf Carts, and Other Low-powered Vehicles
text:
In this subchapter, “neighborhood electric vehicle” means a vehicle that can attain a maximum speed of 35 miles per hour on a paved level surface and otherwise complies with Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standard 500 (49 C.F.R. Section 571.500).

Reenacted by Acts 2011, 82nd Leg., R.S., Ch. 91 (S.B. 1303 ), Sec. 24.014, eff. September 1, 2011.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 551. Operation of Bicycles & Mopeds, Golf Carts, and Other Low-powered Vehicles
##### § 551.301 — Definition

In this subchapter, “neighborhood electric vehicle” means a vehicle that can attain a maximum speed of 35 miles per hour on a paved level surface and otherwise complies with Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standard 500 (49 C.F.R. Section 571.500).

Reenacted by Acts 2011, 82nd Leg., R.S., Ch. 91 (S.B. 1303 ), Sec. 24.014, eff. September 1, 2011.

*Acts 2009, 81st Leg., R.S., Ch. 722 (S.B.129), Sec. 1, eff. September 1, 2009.*
